topStory.text: DigitalOcean announced record Q2 2026 results with multiple nine-figure annual customer commitments for AI inference workloads, secured an additional 20 MW of committed data center capacity for 2027-2028 deployment bringing total committed capacity to approximately 155 MW, and expects revenue growth to accelerate to 29% with Remaining Performance Obligations exceeding $800 million — up more than 10x year over year.

feed.text: Anthropic signed a 20-year, ~$19B lease with TeraWulf for up to 401 MW of AI compute at the Justified Data campus in Hawesville, Kentucky — among the largest single-tenant AI data-center leases disclosed to date, as AI labs race to lock up long-duration capacity ahead of first operations in late 2027.

PJM's heat-wave demand surged to about 163 GW on July 2 — just short of the 2006 all-time record of 165,563 MW — after a July 1 peak of 161,910 MW, the second-highest load in the grid's history, with DOE's first-ever data-center curtailment orders in effect through July 3.

Meta is drawing up plans for a cloud business selling outside access to its AI compute and models — monetizing spare capacity across its multi-gigawatt data-center fleet and putting it into direct competition with AWS, Google Cloud and Microsoft Azure.

National Grid Ventures agreed to invest $1.75B for a 35% stake in Joulent, a US developer of co-located gas, battery and 'across-the-meter' power for large loads — whose first project, a 2.67 GW West Texas gas plant built 50/50 with Chevron, will supply Microsoft's Pecos campus under a 20-year PPA.

Virginia's first-in-the-nation data-center electricity tax took effect July 1: $0.011/kWh on power consumed at data centers, capped at $600M a year (excess refunded pro-rata) and sunsetting July 1, 2028 — a closely watched test of taxing the industry in the world's largest data-center market.

PJM opened the offer window for its 2028/2029 Base Residual Auction on June 30, with results due after 4 p.m. on July 14 — the first capacity auction to run under a FERC-approved price collar of about $325/MW-day (cap) and $175/MW-day (floor); analysts note that without the collar, data-center-driven scarcity could have pushed clearing prices toward roughly $550/MW-day, versus the $333.44 cap the prior 2027/2028 auction hit in December.

Following FERC's June 18 large-load orders, utilities and data-center developers are moving to negotiate 'flexibility-for-speed' interconnection deals — operators accept curtailable or flexible-load terms in exchange for faster grid connections; a 2026 Duke Nicholas Institute study found trimming data-center peak demand just 1–2% could cut electricity rates 0.5–2.8% while protecting reliability.

Microsoft unveiled a multibillion-dollar campus in Pecos (Reeves County), West Texas that will add about 2 GW of capacity — one of its largest single expansions — powered initially by an on-site, behind-the-meter natural-gas plant under a supply deal with Chevron for up to 2.67 GW, bypassing the public grid at launch.
